paper_title: Combined LM and SEM study of the middle Miocene (Sarmatian) palynoflora from the Lavanttal Basin, Austria: Part V. Magnoliophyta 3 – Myrtales to Ericales

methods.description: Investigation of fossil pollen grains using light microscopy (LM) and scanning electron microscopy (SEM) following the single-grain method as described by Zetter (1989) and Halbritter et al. (2018) to capture detailed images of individual grains and compile them for comparison.

steps.description: Investigate fossil pollen grains using light microscopy (LM).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform light microscopy on fossil pollen grains.
outputs: LM images of fossil pollen grains

steps.description: Investigate fossil pollen grains using scanning electron microscopy (SEM) using the single-grain method.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform scanning electron microscopy on fossil pollen grains using the single-grain method.
outputs: SEM images of fossil pollen grains (overview and close-up)

steps.description: Compile pollen micrographs in compiled figures showing the same single pollen grain photographed with LM (sometimes rotated and/or at different focus level) and with SEM (overview and close-up of sculpture).
steps.searchable_summary: Compile and arrange LM and SEM images of the same single pollen grain for comparison.
inputs: LM images of single pollen grains
SEM images of the same single pollen grains
outputs: Compiled figures showing paired LM and SEM images of single pollen grains

methods.method_name: Palaeoclimate and Palaeovegetation Inference using Modern Analogues
methods.description: Method for inferring palaeoclimate and palaeovegetation based on the climatic niches and vegetation associations of potential modern analogues of identified fossil pollen taxa, using Köppen signatures and climate-dependent vegetation categories.

steps.description: Determine fossil pollen taxa from samples.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify and classify fossil pollen grains to determine taxa.
outputs: List of identified fossil pollen taxa

steps.description: Identify potential modern analogues for the determined fossil pollen taxa.
steps.searchable_summary: Find modern plant species or groups that are potential analogues for the identified fossil pollen taxa.
inputs: List of identified fossil pollen taxa
outputs: List of potential modern analogue species/groups

steps.description: Use 'Köppen signatures' (Denk et al. 2013) to summarise the climatic niche occupied by potential modern analogues.
steps.searchable_summary: Assign Köppen climate signatures to potential modern analogue species/groups based on their known distribution and climatic requirements.
inputs: List of potential modern analogue species/groups
outputs: Köppen signatures for each modern analogue

steps.description: Categorise potential modern analogues as climate-dependent vegetation elements adapting the concepts of Schroeder (1998) to provide a more generalised account of the palaeovegetation in relation to modern-day vegetation zones (cf. Denk et al. 2013, figure 2; Grímsson et al. 2016, figure 2).
steps.searchable_summary: Categorize modern analogues into climate-dependent vegetation elements (e.g., nemoral, boreal, etc.) based on their association with modern vegetation zones and adapting established concepts.
inputs: List of potential modern analogue species/groups
outputs: Climate-dependent vegetation element categories for each modern analogue

steps.description: Recognise two additional climate-vegetation categories, the 'oreotropic' and 'austral' elements (Schroeder 1998) to accommodate species with hygric and thermic preferences not covered by the data set used by Denk et al. (2013) and Grímsson et al. (2016), defining their climatic preferences based on Köppen-Geiger (Kottek et al. 2006).
steps.searchable_summary: Define and apply additional climate-vegetation categories ('oreotropic', 'austral') based on specific climatic criteria (Köppen-Geiger) to modern analogues.
inputs: List of potential modern analogue species/groups
outputs: Assignment of modern analogues to 'oreotropic' or 'austral' categories if applicable

steps.description: The climate data for all potential modern analogues of the fossil taxa presented herein are listed in Supplementary File S1.
steps.searchable_summary: Compile and list the climate data (e.g., Köppen signatures, vegetation categories) for all potential modern analogues in a supplementary file.
inputs: List of potential modern analogue species/groups
outputs: Supplementary file (S1) containing climate data for modern analogues
